New Pasco Fire Rescue station opens in Wesley Chapel, more facilities planned as county grows
summary: Pasco County's rapid growth is driving major investments in emergency services. As the county's population has increased by 22% since 2020, Pasco Fire Rescue is expanding its footprint with new stations and modern facilities designed to improve response times and firefighter safety.

title: Police search for men after St. Pete apartment building vandalized
summary: St. Pete police are investigating a case of vandalism at a downtown apartment building over the weekend.

title: Hernando County family, community honor the life of 8-year-old girl killed in accident
summary: Paisley Fratianni-Rollins was about to start third grade next month at J.D. Floyd Elementary School. Her family is now planning her funeral after a tragic accident.

title: Rays slugger Junior Caminero feels fortunate left hand not broken after he was hit in All-Star Game
summary: Junior Caminero feared the worst when a 98 mph sinker that didn’t sink struck him in the left hand in the All-Star Game.

title: Pinellas schools ban smart glasses, tighten e-bike rules for students
summary: Pinellas County students will face new rules this school year that ban smart glasses on campus and require e-bikes to remain outside school buildings.

title: Vandals cause $96K in damage at downtown St. Petersburg tower
summary: Two men tore out landscaping lights and shattered windows at a downtown St. Petersburg residential tower, causing nearly $100,000 in damage, police said.

title: New York puts yearlong moratorium on new data centers, citing energy and climate risks
summary: No large data centers can be built in New York for up to a year as the state creates rules to protect the environment and its energy grid from the power-hungry facilities.

title: House passes bill to make daylight saving time permanent
summary: The House on Tuesday passed with broad bipartisan support legislation that would make daylight saving time permanent nationwide. The lower chamber voted 308-117 for the Sunshine Protection Act of 2025, which would make daylight saving time the permanent standard time. Twenty-two Republicans and 95 Democrats voted against the measure. The legislation now heads to the [...]

summary: Busch Gardens Tampa Bay announced that award-winning coaster, "Kumba," will take its final ride next month as the theme park unveils its new "Kumba's Revenge."

title: Subaru issues recall for 541,000 SUVs over label with incorrect weight rating
summary: Subaru of America is recalling more than 541,000 of its Crosstrek, Forester, and Ascent SUVs due to an inaccurate label attached to the vehicles.

title: Busch Gardens Tampa Bay coaster 'Kumba' to close in August after 33 years
summary: Busch Gardens Tampa Bay announced on July 14 it will be closing its oldest standing roller coaster, Kumba.

title: 'He wanted to protect people': Mother remembers Road Ranger killed on I-75
summary: Investigators say DUI manslaughter suspect had a recent drunk driving arrest and was involved in a hit-and-run earlier the same day.

title: Man struck and killed by truck after fleeing ICE agents in Florida
summary: A 28-year-old man died in Florida Tuesday after he was struck by a semi trailer truck. The man was fleeing from U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement, according to state officials.

title: Energy companies offer programs to help residents save on summer cooling costs
summary: Clearwater residents helped by Duke Energy Tuesday as they connected neighbors to an energy saving program, that's as residents say they're taking a proactive approach to keep cool on a budget.

title: Crystal River man charged after dead puppies found inside home
summary: Deputies investigating a welfare check in Crystal River found dead puppies inside a home filled with odors of decay, feces, and ammonia, leading to felony animal cruelty charges.

title: US reinstates Iranian blockade in the Strait of Hormuz amid ongoing strikes
summary: The U.S. has reinstated an Iranian blockade in the Strait of Hormuz, with 20+ Navy warships now in the region as strikes against Iran continue to threaten a fragile ceasefire.

title: $2.1 million in seized cocaine after 'Hall of Fame' drug bust in Pinellas: PCSO
summary: Pinellas County Sheriff Bob Gualtieri announced Tuesday the seizing of more than $2.1 million in cocaine.

title: State economists: Property tax amendment could cost Florida local governments $11.8 billion annually
summary: Florida’s proposed property tax amendment could eventually reduce local government revenue by nearly $12 billion a year, according to a new analysis from the state’s Revenue Estimating Conference.
